entry.title: Hands-on Project: Using GNS3 to Simulate Three-tier Architecture Networking for Small and Medium-sized Enterprises
entry.content:
Install GNS3

  • Installation tutorial: https://blog.csdn.net/qq_63855540/article/details/147834429

    (GNS3’s default terminal is ugly; it’s recommended to switch to one you’re used to, like MobaXterm)

  • Virtualization environment error: https://henchat.net/win11-24h2-vmware-error-intel-vt-x-ept/

  • Stuck on waiting http://local:3080: https://tieba.baidu.com/p/6059085630

    Execute the following two commands in cmd, then restart
    netstat -anb
    netsh winsock reset

  • Cisco Image 1: https://github.com/hegdepavankumar/Cisco-Images-for-GNS3-and-EVE-NG

  • Cisco Image 2: https://zhaokaifeng.com/6069/

Requirements

The problem requires designing a network architecture with security protection for a medium-sized enterprise with 200 employees. The company has three departments: sales, development, and management. It needs to provide internal network remote access, and a DMZ to host web, email, and DNS servers. The network architecture uses a three-layer structure: core, distribution, and access, utilizing two or more L3 switches. Appropriate VLANs and IP ranges need to be designed. Internal/external firewalls, routers, and switches need security configuration and access control. Access endpoints include employee devices and guest APs. Cloud simulation will be done using virtual machine network configuration, and Kali will be used to simulate a public network attacker for security testing.

Network Design

A redundant design would better meet the requirements, but due to time constraints, this design only implements a structure with two L3 switches serving as the core and distribution layers, respectively. Although a single L3 switch can perform both core and distribution functions, separating them can improve processing efficiency and better align with real-world needs. The core layer focuses on high-speed, reliable data forwarding and is deployed in a central data center (e.g., a school’s network center), while the distribution layer is responsible for aggregating traffic from multiple L2 switches (access layer), routing for VLANs, and enabling ACLs (Access Control Lists) to control VLAN access permissions, deployed in departmental server rooms (e.g., a dormitory building’s main server room).

Three-layer analysis reference: https://libai.blog.csdn.net/article/details/151764710

For security design, a three-legged firewall architecture is used here, where the DMZ switch connects directly to the firewall, protecting communication between the internal network and the DMZ. However, this may degrade performance for internal hosts accessing DMZ services, as traffic must pass through the firewall. Additionally, a dual firewall solution exists, with an external firewall filtering traffic entering from the public network, and an internal firewall protecting communication between the DMZ and the internal network.

Firewall structure design reference: https://www.firewall.cx/networking/network-fundamentals/firewall-topologies-dmz-zone.html

DMZ, Demilitarized Zone, is an isolated area used to deploy servers accessible from both internal and external networks. Because the services deployed face untrusted external traffic, the DMZ itself becomes “contaminated” and untrusted, while also needing to provide services to the internal network. To safely isolate internal and external network traffic, and prevent external traffic from directly penetrating the servers to reach the internal network, a DMZ is necessary. Even if a server in the DMZ is compromised, it cannot directly move laterally into the internal network.

Device Selection (Image Pitfalls)

  • Cloud

    Here, I simulate the public network on my local machine; it does not actually communicate with the real internet. In VMware’s virtual network editor, select a vmnet, set it to host-only mode, and assign it a subnet IP. Add a network card to the GNS3 VM, connect it to this vmnet. I also use Kali to simulate a public network intruder, so Kali is also connected to this vmnet.

  • External Firewall

    pfSense serves as both an external router and firewall, and VPN configuration is convenient. Download the pfSense image from the official website, then create a new virtual machine in edit>preferences>Qemu VMs and import the downloaded image. Parts not explicitly shown in screenshots can be left as default.

    Allocate 2G RAM, otherwise it won’t run.

    Select the downloaded image.

    Modify the number of interfaces as needed:

  • L3 Switch (core, distribution-switch)

    Select the c3725-adventerprisek9- mz.124-15.T14 image and import it into IOS routers.

    Because the requirement is to enable security protection features, many L3 switches were searched but none had this function, so a c3725 router is used to simulate an L3 switch. Reference: https://blog.csdn.net/woay2008/article/details/124045710

  • L2 Switch (DMZ switch, sales/dev/mgmt/guest-switch, guest AP)

    Select the viosl2-adventerprisek9-m.ssa.high-iron-20200929 image, similar to pfSense, import it into a QEMU VM, and keep all settings as default.

    The guest AP is also simulated using an L2 switch.

  • DMZ Servers

    All servers in the DMZ use Docker: python:3-alpine.

    (Because I encountered various issues with Docker images for individual services)

    In docker containers, click new, select new image, and enter the Docker image name above.

  • User PC

    To test network connectivity and call DMZ services, a Docker image with network tools is used: docker:praqma/network-multitool.

  • Browser for pfSense configuration

    pfSense has a web interface, so to facilitate configuration, pull out a machine and connect it to pfSense. Choose docker:jlesage/firefox.

Note: Images may not have the features you want; try a few different ones.

Network Segmentation

  • Key IPs

    pfSense: 10.0.254.1

    Core L3 f1/1: 10.0.254.2

    Core L3 f1/0: 10.0.253.2

    Distribution L3: 10.0.253.1

  • DMZ

    VLAN 99

    Subnet: 10.0.99.0/24

    Gateway (pfSense interface e3 connected to DMZ): 10.0.99.1

    Web: 10.0.99.10

    DNS: 10.0.99.20

    Mail: 10.0.99.30

  • Sales Dept.

    VLAN 10

    Subnet: 10.0.10.0/24

    Gateway (Distribution L3 interface connected to Sales Dept. switch): 10.0.10.2

    Reserved addresses: 10.0.10.1-10.0.10.10

    DHCP pool: 10.0.10.10-10.0.10.254

  • Development Dept.

    VLAN 20

    Subnet: 10.0.20.0/24

    Gateway (Distribution L3 interface connected to Development Dept. switch): 10.0.20.2

    Reserved addresses: 10.0.20.1-10.0.20.10

    DHCP pool: 10.0.20.10-10.0.20.254

  • Management Dept.

    VLAN 30

    Subnet: 10.0.30.0/24

    Gateway (Distribution L3 interface connected to Management Dept. switch): 10.0.30.2

    Reserved addresses: 10.0.30.1-10.0.30.10

    DHCP pool: 10.0.30.10-10.0.30.254

  • Guest WiFi

    VLAN 50

    Subnet: 10.0.50.0/24

    Gateway (Distribution L3 interface connected to Guest switch): 10.0.50.2

    Reserved addresses: 10.0.50.1-10.0.50.10

    DHCP pool: 10.0.50.10-10.0.50.254

  • OpenVPN (configured on pfSense)

    Subnet: 10.0.8.0/24

Configure L2 Switches

Assign VLANs, connect L3 switch and user terminals:

If ARP protection is to be added to L2: (First disable option 82)

Check ARP protection settings; if configured correctly, it will look similar to the images below:

Configure Distribution L3 Switch

Configure connection to Core L3:

Create VLANs:

Configure DHCP:

ACL:

Configure Core L3 Switch

Configure static routes:

Internal Network Connectivity Test

User PC IP acquisition:

Example of successful IP acquisition:

After all devices have acquired IPs, ping each other to test connectivity.

When pinging other devices from a switch, “!” indicates success.

Configure pfSense

Pull out a Firefox image and connect it to pfSense. If you close and then reopen this Firefox device, the port will be occupied and you won’t be able to open the web page; you’ll need to delete the device and pull out a new one to connect.

After pulling out Firefox, it won’t have an IP. You need to enter the Firefox Docker shell in the VMware GNS3 VM and configure it:

System -> Routing -> Static Routes: Add a static route: point the next hop (Gateway) for the internal network segment 10.0.0.0/16 to the Core Switch’s interface IP: 10.0.254.2

Configure DMZ Servers

  • Web Server

    After dragging it out, double-click to enter settings, fill in the start command. The web service will start when the device starts.

    sh -lc "ip addr add 10.0.99.10/24 dev eth0; ip link set eth0 up; ip route replace default via 10.0.99.1; mkdir -p /srv/www; echo 'DMZ WEB OK' > /srv/www/index.html; cd /srv/www; exec python3 -m http.server 80 --bind 0.0.0.0"

  • DNS Server

    Connect to a Cloud interface that can access the public network to download:

    Connect to the DMZ switch, then edit the configuration file and start the service:

    Test commands for clients:

  • Mail Server

    Connect to a Cloud interface that can access the public network to download:

    Connect to DMZ:

    Test:

Strange Errors

If GNS3 runs for a long time, situations may arise where configurations seem correct but connectivity fails. You can right-click on the link to capture packets for inspection. Sometimes, the network cable (the connection between two devices) appears connected but is actually broken, or a device’s network port is acting up, etc. When in doubt, restart GNS3; it saves a lot of time. (I spent half a day troubleshooting DHCP before a restart fixed it, infuriating.)

Improvements

  1. There is only one aggregation and distribution switch, so all traffic is forwarded through a single L3 switch, which limits performance and presents a single point of failure risk (if one fails, everything goes down). Given enough time, a redundant structure should be implemented. In a real redundant architecture, the distribution layer typically has two L3 switches configured with HSRP (Hot Standby Router Protocol) or VRRP (Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol) to create a unified virtual gateway IP (e.g., 10.0.10.1). This way, if one distribution switch goes down, the other instantly takes over the gateway without any disruption to user devices.
  2. Although some security settings have been implemented, they are very basic and only meet the needs of learning and experimentation. In actual applications, advanced detection and defense systems like IDS should be used.

entry.title: 【项目实践】使用GNS3模拟三层架构中小型企业组网
entry.content:
安装GNS3

  • 安装教程：https://blog.csdn.net/qq_63855540/article/details/147834429

    （gns3默认的终端难看，建议换个自己习惯的，比如mobaxterm）

  • 虚拟化环境报错：https://henchat.net/win11-24h2-vmware-error-intel-vt-x-ept/

  • 一直在waiting http://local:3080：https://tieba.baidu.com/p/6059085630

    cmd下执行以下两行命令，然后重启
    netstat -anb
    netsh winsock reset

  • 思科镜像1：https://github.com/hegdepavankumar/Cisco-Images-for-GNS3-and-EVE-NG

  • 思科镜像2：https://zhaokaifeng.com/6069/

要求

题目要求为200名员工的中型企业设计一个具有安全防护功能的网络架构，公司有销售、开发、管理三个部门，需要提供内网远程访问，DMZ托管web、邮箱、DNS服务器。网络架构使用三层结构：核心（core），汇聚（distribute），接入（access），使用两个及以上三层交换机。设计适当的VLAN、IP范围。内/外部防火墙，路由器，交换机做安全配置和访问控制。接入端包括员工设备和访客AP。用虚拟机网络配置模拟云端，使用kali接入模拟公网攻击者，进行安全测试。

网络设计

其实用冗余设计来满足题目要求更好，但由于时间限制，这里只设计并实现了由两个三层交换机分别作为核心、汇聚层的结构。虽然一个三层交换机也能同时承担核心和汇聚功能，但分开来能提高处理效率，也更符合现实需求。核心层专注于高速、可靠地转发数据，部署在中心机房（比如学校网络中心），而汇聚层负责汇聚多台二层交换机（接入层）的流量，给VLAN做路由，能启用ACL（Access Control List，访问控制）控制VLAN的访问权限，部署在分属的机房（比如宿舍楼的总机房）。

三层解析参考：https://libai.blog.csdn.net/article/details/151764710

在安全设计上，这里使用三脚防火墙three-legged firewall的架构，DMZ交换机直接连接在防火墙上，使得内网与DMZ之间的通信被防火墙保护。但会使得内部主机访问DMZ 服务性能下降，因为要经过防火墙才能访问。此外还有双防火墙方案，外部防火墙用于过滤从公网进入的流量，内部防火墙保护DMZ和内部网络之间的通信。

防火墙结构设计参考：https://www.firewall.cx/networking/network-fundamentals/firewall-topologies-dmz-zone.html

DMZ，Demilitarized Zone，是一个用来部署供内外网访问的服务器的隔离区，因为部署的服务要面对外部不可信流量，所以它也受到“污染”，变得不可信，而同时它也要为内部网络提供服务。为了将内外网流量安全隔离开来，外网流量无法直接穿透服务器抵达内网，就需要使用DMZ，即使DMZ中的服务器被攻破，也无法直接横向移动到内网。

设备选择（镜像坑）

  • Cloud

    我这里在本机上模拟公网，实际上并不与真实互联网互通。在vmware的虚拟网络编辑器里选择一个vmnet，设置为仅主机模式，自己设置一个子网ip给它。给gns3 vm添加一个网卡，接入这个vmnet，我还使用kali模拟公网的入侵者，所以给kali也接入这个vmnet。

  • 外部防火墙

    pfsense同时满足外部路由器和防火墙的功能，VPN配置方便。在官网上下载pfsense镜像，从edit>preferences>Qemu VMs中新建一个虚拟机，将下载的镜像导入，没有截图的部分默认即可。

    给2G RAM，否则运行不了。

    选择下载的镜像。

    根据自己需要修改接口数量：

  • L3交换机（core、distribution-switch）

    选择c3725-adventerprisek9- mz.124-15.T14镜像，在IOS routers导入。

    因为要求启用安全防护功能，找了很多L3交换机都没有这个功能，所以用c3725路由器来模拟L3交换机。参考：https://blog.csdn.net/woay2008/article/details/124045710

  • L2交换机（DMZ交换机、sales/dev/mgmt/guest-switch、访客AP）

    选择viosl2-adventerprisek9-m.ssa.high-iron-20200929镜像，跟pfsense类似，在QEMU VM中导入，设置全都默认。

    访客AP也用L2交换机模拟。

  • DMZ服务器

    DMZ中的服务器全都使用docker：python:3-alpine。

    （因为我用各个服务的docker镜像都遇到各种问题）

    在docker containers里new，选择new image，输入上述docker名即可。

  • 用户PC

    因为要测试网络连通性和调用DMZ服务，用了一个有网络工具的docker。docker：praqma/network-multitool

  • 配置pfsense的浏览器

    pfsense有web端，为了方便配置，拉一台机器出来连上pfsense。选docker：jlesage/firefox

注意项：镜像不一定有你想要的功能，多换几个试试

网段划分

  • 关键IP

    pfsense：10.0.254.1

    核心L3 f1/1：10.0.254.2

    核心L3 f1/0：10.0.253.2

    汇聚L3：10.0.253.1

  • DMZ

    vlan 99

    子网：10.0.99.0/24

    网关（pfsense与dmz连接的接口e3）：10.0.99.1

    web：10.0.99.10

    dns：10.0.99.20

    mail：10.0.99.30

  • 销售部

    vlan 10

    子网：10.0.10.0/24

    网关（汇聚L3与销售部交换机的接口）：10.0.10.2

    保留地址：10.0.10.1-10.0.10.10

    DHCP池：10.0.10.10-10.0.10.254

  • 开发部

    vlan 20

    子网：10.0.20.0/24

    网关（汇聚L3与开发部交换机的接口）：10.0.20.2

    保留地址：10.0.20.1-10.0.20.10

    DHCP池：10.0.20.10-10.0.20.254

  • 管理部

    vlan 30

    子网：10.0.30.0/24

    网关（汇聚L3与管理部交换机的接口）：10.0.30.2

    保留地址：10.0.30.1-10.0.30.10

    DHCP池：10.0.30.10-10.0.30.254

  • 访客wifi

    vlan 50

    子网：10.0.50.0/24

    网关（汇聚L3与访客交换机的接口）：10.0.50.2

    保留地址：10.0.50.1-10.0.50.10

    DHCP池：10.0.50.10-10.0.50.254

  • OpenVPN（pfsense上配置）

    子网：10.0.8.0/24

配置二层交换机（L2）

分配vlan，连通三层交换机和用户终端：

若二层要加上arp防护：（先关闭option 82）

检查arp防护设置，设置正确会类似下图：

配置汇聚三层交换机（distribution L3）

配置与核心L3的连接：

创建vlan：

配置DHCP:

ACL：

配置核心三层交换机（core L3）

配置静态路由：

内网连通测试

用户PC获取ip：

成功拿到ip示例：

都获取了ip之后互相ping一下测试能否连通。

在交换机上ping其他设备，显示“!”才是通了

配置pfsense

拉一个firefox镜像出来连上pfsense，如果关闭这个firefox设备再开启它，端口会被占用没法打开网页，需要删掉这个设备重新拉出来连接。

firefox拉出来之后是没有ip的，要在VMware的gns3 vm里，进入firefox docker的shell设置：

System -> Routing -> Static Routes：添加一条静态路由：将内网网段 10.0.0.0/16的下一跳（Gateway）指向核心交换机的接口 IP：10.0.254.2

配置DMZ服务器

  • web服务器

    拖出来之后双击进设置，填start command，启动设备时web服务也随之启动。

    sh -lc "ip addr add 10.0.99.10/24 dev eth0; ip link set eth0 up; ip route replace default via 10.0.99.1; mkdir -p /srv/www; echo 'DMZ WEB OK' > /srv/www/index.html; cd /srv/www; exec python3 -m http.server 80 --bind 0.0.0.0"

  • DNS服务器

    接Cloud上一个能访问公网的接口下载

    接dmz交换机，这里在编辑配置文件并启动服务：

    各客户端测试命令：

  • Mail服务器

    接Cloud上一个能访问公网的接口下载：

    接DMZ:

    测试：

奇怪的错误

gns3开久了会出现明明配置没错，却死活打不通的情况，可以在连线上右键抓包检查。有时候网线（两个设备间连的线）连着但实际上断了，设备网口抽风了，等等。遇事不决重启一下gns3，节省很多时间。(调DHCP时调了大半天最后重启好了，气疯了)

改进

  1. 汇聚和分发交换机只有一个，流量都通过一个三层交换机转发，性能上受限制，存在单点故障风险（一个坏了全都宕机），如果时间足够的话，应该实现一个冗余结构。在真实的冗余架构中，汇聚层通常会有两台三层交换机，通过配置 HSRP（热备份路由器协议） 或 VRRP（虚拟路由器冗余协议） 虚拟出一个统一的网关 IP（如 10.0.10.1）。这样当一台汇聚交换机宕机时，另一台会瞬间接管网关，用户端完全无感知。
  2. 虽然实现了一些安全设置，但非常基本，只能满足学习和实验的需要，实际应用中要采用IDS等高级检测防御系统。

entry.title: [Learning Notes] I2P Overview
entry.content:
Introduction

I2P (Invisible Internet Project) is a decentralized peer-to-peer (P2P) anonymous network, developed in Java. Similar to Tor, it uses several network nodes to encrypt and transmit data, but offers a higher degree of anonymity than Tor.
Tor primarily focuses on providing users with anonymous access to external internet services, addressing “anonymity” and “hidden services,” emphasizing application layer design, and building an “overlay network.”
I2P attempts to migrate existing internet services to the I2P network and provide service implementations within its framework, offering anonymous file sharing and anonymous web hosting, addressing “anonymity,” “hidden services,” and “security,” emphasizing network layer design, and building a “virtual internet.”
(Reference: https://www.hanspub.org/journal/paperinformation?paperID=31299&btwaf=39457632)

Usage

  1. Installation
    Requires Java version higher than 1.5.
    Installation guides can be found on the official website: https://geti2p.net/en/download
    (Official note: Do not start I2P as root on Linux)

  2. Connecting to the I2P network
    The first-time connection to the I2P network requires reseed, which will be detailed later.

  3. Setting up a proxy
    Accessing websites through I2P requires setting up a local proxy. The default HTTP proxy port is 4444, and HTTPS is 4445.

After successful launch, visiting 127.0.0.1:7657 will display the I2P console, including settings and various network metrics.

Network metrics:

Mechanism

  1. Kad algorithm
    Purpose: To acquire network node information by spreading it through seed nodes, and to break down raw data into encrypted packets for transmission through multiple cross-routed tunnels.

Features:
① Only a small portion of the entire I2P network nodes are obtained.
② No directory servers are needed.
③ Every host running I2P is a network relay, sharing bandwidth to forward data for others.
④ Once connected to I2P, it continuously acquires information about some other nodes in the network, ensuring connectivity with the I2P network.

  1. Network database netdb

  1. Garlic Routing
    When transmitting information, the information is first divided into multiple sub-messages. Then, available intermediate nodes are randomly selected from the network database (netdb) to build multiple encrypted tunnels.

    ① Tunnels
    When establishing a tunnel, the tunnel initiator must send requests and necessary configuration data to each hop selected by the initiator, and wait for confirmation from each hop before starting to build the tunnel. The tunnel establishment request sent by the initiator is encrypted, and only the tunnel creator can access the confirmation information from each hop. The maximum tunnel lifetime is 10 minutes. Typically, a tunnel has 3 hops: a gateway node, intermediate nodes (adjustable quantity), and an endpoint. A complete unidirectional transmission includes an outbound tunnel and an inbound tunnel, involving at least 6 nodes.

    ② Peer selection
    In selecting available intermediate nodes, I2P clients rely on previously monitored performance values and the current state of the network, without using active bandwidth probing. The I2P node selection algorithm can react very quickly to failed peers and other changes in network topology, selecting peers by continuously analyzing and ranking performance rather than trusting claimed capacity.
    (Reference: http://www.3n1ac.com/index.php/archives/3/)

    ③ Tunnel creation
    When a routing node wants to create an outbound tunnel, it first checks its netDb folder to obtain a series of RouterInfo messages (this information is obtained from real-time monitoring by the router). It then selects several nodes based on their performance and other metrics, strictly sorts them according to a certain algorithm, configures the corresponding information for each selected node, and then forwards it.

    For an outbound tunnel, the tunnel creator is the tunnel gateway. It forwards the tunnel creation command and other pre-configured information in the previously selected and sorted order to the next hop router—the intermediate node of the tunnel. The intermediate node, upon receiving the command, understands that a tunnel needs to be created. It continues to forward the command and then replaces the request record with a reply record (indicating whether it agrees to participate in the tunnel), continuing to forward to the next hop. When the message is forwarded all the way to the last node of the tunnel, i.e., the endpoint, it will forward the message to the corresponding inbound tunnel according to the previous command, and finally return to the corresponding tunnel creator.

    Since tunnel construction incurs significant time costs and bandwidth load, and each tunnel has a maximum lifetime of 10 minutes, after which it is destroyed, I2P routers maintain a series of tunnel pools to ensure efficient service operation and provide redundant bandwidth for applications. Each tunnel in a pool is used for the same communication purpose.

    ④ Endpoint processing
    After the last hop of the tunnel receives and verifies the tunnel message, how the endpoint recovers the gateway’s data depends on whether the tunnel is an inbound or an outbound tunnel.
    For an outbound tunnel, the endpoint encrypts and forwards the message using its layer key, just like other participants.
    For an inbound tunnel, the endpoint is also the tunnel creator. Therefore, they can iteratively decrypt the IV key and message using the layer encryption keys and IV (tunnel initialization vector) keys in the reverse order of the tunnel node routing.

I2P vs. TOR Comparison

  1. I2P Garlic Routing
    ① Fully decentralized, node information is stored in each I2P node; no directory servers are needed.
    ② Multiple tunnels transmit received and sent information, and they change every 10 minutes.
    ③ Transmitted information is split into multiple sub-messages and sent to the destination via different encrypted tunnels.
    ④ Slower speed.
  2. TOR Onion Routing
    ① Relies on directory servers to obtain relay nodes, easily blocked.
    ② A single tunnel transmits received and sent information.
    ③ Faster than I2P.

Reseed

Due to the Kad algorithm requiring initial seeds to propagate and acquire more node information, reseed is necessary when connecting to the I2P network for the first time. This involves utilizing a certain number of known active nodes to discover other nodes.
Once connected to I2P, connectivity can be maintained. If unused for a period (over three months), reseed is required again due to the lack of up-to-date active I2P node information.
I2P initially includes built-in seed node information, but it may become invalid, necessitating other reseed methods.

  • Reseed websites, files
    I2P has several built-in reseed websites, which are the default reseed method.
    Reseed files can also be downloaded from reseed websites, requiring high timeliness.
    You can create and share reseed files with others (node information comes from your own network database).

    Attached are some reseed websites:
    https://banana.incognet.io/
    https://i2p.novg.net/
    https://i2pseed.creativecowpat.net:8443/
    https://reseed-fr.i2pd.xyz/
    https://reseed-pl.i2pd.xyz/
    https://reseed.diva.exchange/
    https://reseed.i2pgit.org/
    https://reseed.memcpy.io/
    https://reseed.onion.im/
    https://reseed2.i2p.net/
    https://app.box.com/s/aednqugd5zf07mlg65wjeafay3b1qqbg/folder/88436877456
    https://www2.mk16.de/
    https://i2phides.me/i2pseeds.su3

  • Reseed files
    Importing from files supports zip and su3 formats; su3 is a compressed file with signature capability.
    Upon decompression, opening with Notepad reveals I2P router information, including host, port, and key information for both NTCP and SSU protocols, which corresponds to the content displayed in the netdb of the console. The garbled part is the su3 file header information; a detailed table can be found in the official documentation.
    (Official documentation: https://geti2p.net/spec/updates#su3)
    (According to incomplete observation, about 70 new ones are added per hour)

Websites

I2P anonymous sites (eepsites) are websites within the I2P network. I2P site names end with .i2p, and these sites are found by identifying keys (i.e., encrypted base64 keys, different from common base64, using ~ instead of / and - instead of +). Known site keys are stored in the local host.txt file.

  • Address book
    I2P website keys can be accessed by subscribing to address books, navigating websites, or importing addresses manually.
    For example:
    http://tino.i2p/hosts.txt
    http://stats.i2p/cgi-bin/newhosts.txt
    http://i2host.i2p/cgi-bin/i2hostetag
    http://no.i2p/export/alive-hosts.txt
    http://notbob.i2p/hosts.txt
    Taking notbob as an example, website categories include search engines, cryptocurrencies and trading, git, video, sqbl, etc.

  • Websites
    When visiting a website not in the address book for the first time, a jump service can be used to find its key.

I2P vs. TOR Terminology Comparison

entry.title: 【学习笔记】I2P概述
entry.content:
简介

I2P（Invisible Internet Project ）是去中心化的点到点（P2P）匿名网络，使用JAVA开发。与Tor类似，都是通过若干个网络节点来加密和传输数据，但匿名度比Tor更高。
Tor主要致力于为用户提供匿名访问实施和操作外部的互联网服务，解决“匿名性”和“隐藏服务”，侧重应用层设计，构建了一个“覆盖网络”。
I2P试图将现有互联网服务转移到I2P网络，并在框架内提供服务实现，提供匿名文件共享和匿名网络托管，解决“匿名性”、“隐藏服务”和“安全性”，侧重网络层设计，构建了一个“虚拟互联网”。
（参考：https://www.hanspub.org/journal/paperinformation?paperID=31299&btwaf=39457632）

使用

  1. 安装
    需要安装高于1.5的JAVA版本
    安装教程可以在官网找到，https://geti2p.net/en/download
    （官方注：在linux上不要用root启动i2p）

  2. 连接I2P网络
    首次连接I2P网络需要补种，后面详细说。

  3. 设置代理
    通过I2P访问网站需要设置本地代理，默认HTTP代理端口为4444，HTTPS为4445。

启动成功后，访问127.0.0.1:7657就能看到I2P的控制台，包括设置和各项网络指标。

网络指标：

机制

  1. Kad算法
    用途：通过种子节点扩散获取网络节点信息、将传输的原始数据拆散为加密数据包通过多条隧道交叉疏散传递。

特点：
① 拿到的只是整个I2P网络的一小部分节点
② 不需要目录服务器
③ 每一台运行I2P的主机都是网络中继，共享带宽帮别人转发数据
④ 只要连上一次I2P，就能不断获取网络中部分其他节点的信息，保证与I2P网络维持连接

  1. 网络数据库netdb

  1. 大蒜路由
    要传输信息时，首先将要传输的信息分成多个子信息，然后从网络数据库（netdb）中随机选取可用的中间节点，构建多个加密隧道。

    ① 隧道
    建立隧道时，隧道的发起者必须向发起者选出来的隧道的每一跳发送请求和必要的配置数据，并且等到每一跳的确认信息后才能开始建立隧道。隧道的发起者发送的建立隧道请求是加密的，并且只有隧道的创建者才能够访问每一跳的确认信息。隧道最大生命周期为10分钟。通常情况下，一条隧道有3跳，即网关节点、中间节点（可调整数量）、终点。一个完整的单向传输包括输出隧道和输入隧道，至少有6个节点参与其中。

    ② 对等实体（peer）选择
    在选取可用中间节点中， I2P客户端依赖于先前监控的性能值和网络的当前状态，不使用有效带宽探测。I2P节点选择算法，能够非常快速地对失败的对等体和网络拓扑中的其他变化做出反应，通过不断分析和排名性能来选择对等体，而不是信任所声称的容量。
    （参考：http://www.3n1ac.com/index.php/archives/3/）

    ③ 隧道创建
    当一个路由节点想要创建一条输出隧道的时候，它会首先查看自己的netDb文件夹，获取一系列的RouterInfor信息（这个信息是之前路由实时监测得到的），根据他们的性能和其他指标从其中挑选几个节点，并且将节点根据一定的算法进行严格的排序，之后为挑选出来的每一个节点配置好相应的信息，然后转发出去。

    对于输出隧道来讲，隧道创建者就是隧道网关，将创建隧道的指令等之前配置好的信息按照之前挑选出来的、排好的顺序转发下一跳路由——隧道的中间节点，中间节点接收到指令之后，会了解到现在要创建一条隧道，它将指令会继续转发下去，然后会将请求记录替换成回复记录（说明是否同意参与隧道），继续转发到下一跳当中去。当消息一直转发到隧道的最后一个节点，即终点的时候，会按照之前的指令将消息转发到相应的输入隧道中去，最终返回到对应隧道创建者那里。

    由于隧道的构建会有较大的时间成本和带宽负载，并且每条隧道有最高10分钟的生命周期，超过这个时间之后隧道就会被销毁，所以为了实现服务的高效运行，为应用提供冗余的带宽，每个I2P路由器都会维护一系列的隧道池，每个隧道池中的隧道用于同一种通信目的。

    ④ 终点处理
    在隧道的最后一跳接收并验证了隧道消息之后，终点如何恢复网关的数据同样取决于隧道是输入隧道还是输出隧道。
    对于输出隧道，终点像其他参与者一样，使用其层密钥对消息进行加密、转发即可。
    对于输入隧道，终点也是隧道创建者，因此它们可以使用与隧道节点路由的顺序相反的顺序，使用每层加密密钥和IV（隧道初始化向量）密钥迭代地解密IV密钥和消息。

I2P与TOR对比

  1. I2P 大蒜路由
    ① 完全去中心化，节点信息被保存在每一个I2P节点中，不需要目录服务器
    ② 多条隧道传输接收和发送的信息，且每10分钟更换一次
    ③ 传输的信息会被切分为多个子信息，通过不同加密隧道传输到目的地
    ④ 速度慢
  2. TOR 洋葱路由
    ① 依赖目录服务器获取中继节点，容易被封禁
    ② 单条隧道传输接收和发送的信息
    ③ 比I2P快

补种

由于Kad算法需要初始的种子来扩散获取更多节点信息，在初次连接I2P网络时需要补种，即利用一定数量的已知活跃节点来寻找其他节点。
一旦连上了I2P，就能一直联网，若一段时间不使用（超过三个月），由于没有了最新的活跃I2P节点信息，才需要重新补种。
I2P初始内置了种子节点信息，但有可能无效，需要其他方法补种。

  • 补种网站、文件
    I2P内置了几个补种网站，是默认补种方法。
    也可以自行在补种网站上下载补种文件，要求时效性高。
    自己可以创建补种文件分享给他人（节点信息来源于自身网络数据库）

    附部分补种网站：
    https://banana.incognet.io/
    https://i2p.novg.net/
    https://i2pseed.creativecowpat.net:8443/
    https://reseed-fr.i2pd.xyz/
    https://reseed-pl.i2pd.xyz/
    https://reseed.diva.exchange/
    https://reseed.i2pgit.org/
    https://reseed.memcpy.io/
    https://reseed.onion.im/
    https://reseed2.i2p.net/
    https://app.box.com/s/aednqugd5zf07mlg65wjeafay3b1qqbg/folder/88436877456
    https://www2.mk16.de/
    https://i2phides.me/i2pseeds.su3

  • 补种文件
    从文件导入有zip和su3格式，su3是带有签名功能的压缩文件。
    解压后记事本打开可以看到一个I2P路由信息，包括NTCP和SSU两个协议的host、端口、密钥等信息，即控制台中netdb所显示的内容。乱码部分是su3的文件头信息，详细表格见官方文档。
    （官方文档：https://geti2p.net/spec/updates#su3）
    （根据不完全观察，每小时新增70个左右）

网站

I2P匿名站点（eepsite）是I2P网络内的网站。I2P站点的名称以.i2p结尾，通过识别密钥（即加密后的base64密钥，不同于常见base64，用~代替/，用-代替+）寻找这些站点，已知站点密钥保存在本地host.txt文件中。

  • 地址簿
    I2P的网站密钥可以通过订阅地址簿、导航网站、自行导入地址访问。
    如：
    http://tino.i2p/hosts.txt
    http://stats.i2p/cgi-bin/newhosts.txt
    http://i2host.i2p/cgi-bin/i2hostetag
    http://no.i2p/export/alive-hosts.txt
    http://notbob.i2p/hosts.txt
    以notbob为例，网站分类有搜索引擎、加密货币与交易、git、视频、sqbl等。

  • 网站
    首次访问未在地址簿中的网站时，可以通过跳转服务来找到该网站的密钥。

I2P与TOR术语对比

entry.title: 【Learning Notes】Communication Characteristics of Six RAT (Remote Access Trojan) Protocols
entry.content:
1 Gh0st

1.1 Overview

Gh0stRAT was initially developed by the C.Rufus team. The client runs as a service, can auto-start with the system, and evades antivirus software detection by hiding files and processes. It primarily includes modules for file management, system information collection, process management, screenshots, and remote control. Additionally, it has capabilities for stealing passwords, capturing network traffic, and executing remote CMD commands. It typically spreads through email attachments, malicious websites, or software vulnerabilities.

This Trojan was first discovered in 2009. Its 3.6 version source code was freely available online, making it popular and leading to many modified variants, such as Dahuitun. In 2018, the Iron Tiger group was observed using a Gh0stRAT variant in attack campaigns. In 2021, a Gh0stRAT variant was detected implanting backdoors on user computers via a fake Telegram installer.

1.2 Basic Features

  1. TCP transmission, default listening port is 80, commonly found on ports 8000 and 443, usually masquerades as a Microsoft Device Manager service.
  2. The data (payload) header displays a magic number in plain text. Examples: Gh0st, GOLDt, HEART, Level, BeiJi.
  3. A fixed magic number will expose its version information.
  4. It will also respond to illegal requests outside of some protocol specifications, such as GET / HTTP/1.0 .
  5. Lacks effective packet validation methods.
  6. No reasonable session management mechanism.
  7. In the source code, the m_iocpServer() method is responsible for communication and sending packets. Although Gh0stRAT has multiple variants, this method is a core part of each kernel and is usually not modified, so all variants share a unified communication characteristic.

1.3 Communication Characteristics

Environment setup reference: gh0st3.6 + vc6.0 + windows xp compilation process

Throughout the entire communication process, the TCP flags of traffic packets used for data transmission all include PSH. In the Gh0stRAT handshake, the controlled host first initiates a TCP connection request. After establishing a TCP connection through a three-way handshake, the controlled host sends a payload prefixed with “Gh0st\xae\x00\x00\x00\xe0\x00\x00\x00\x78\x9c“, containing its encoded and obfuscated system information for going online.

Upon receiving the online information, the master client responds with a confirmation traffic packet. The Payload is also prefixed with “Gh0st \xae\x00\x00\x00\xe0\x00\x00\x00\x78\x9c“. Bytes 14-15, “\x78\x9c“, are the zlib compression header, fixed in position within Gh0st communication packets, see Analysis of Gh0st Dahuitun RAT Family Communication Protocol.

2 DarkcometRAT

2.1 Overview

DarkcometRAT was developed by Jean-Pierre Lesueur in 2008, also known as “Dark Comet”. Besides common encryption and obfuscation techniques found in remote access Trojans, it also includes rootkit functionality, allowing it to hide its presence in the system. In 2012, the Trojan’s author stopped updating it, with the latest version being 5.4.1.

Currently active Trojans mostly use dynamic domain names as server domains, primarily “ddns.net” and “duckdns.org”, accounting for over 50%. In a recent monthly threat intelligence status report by Tencent Anti-Virus Lab, Darkcomet was found to be the most used malicious network protocol that month, with a single-day peak of 3000 instances.

2.2 Basic Features

  1. TCP transmission, default listening port is 1604.
  2. RC4 algorithm is used for encryption and decryption of communication traffic; the master client and controlled host need to use the same key to receive and send information.
  3. Uses predefined command words for communication, with parameters separated by “|”.
  5. TCP flags of traffic packets involved in data transmission all include PSH.

2.3 Communication Characteristics

After completing the TCP three-way handshake, the master client sends the command word “IDTYPE“, encrypted with the pre-set key using the RC4 algorithm.

Default keys are version-dependent and all in the format “#KCMDDC𝑥#-8900123456789“, where 𝑥 is the version number, and “0123456789“ is a user-changeable field.

The specific correspondence is as follows:

Subsequently, the controlled host responds with the ciphertext of the command word “SERVER“. After the master client receives it, it responds with the command word “GetSIN“, requesting the controlled host’s information and attaching the IP address of the database that will receive the information. The controlled host then responds with its own information, completing the Darkcomet handshake.

2.4 Platform Comparison

shodan: Searching for darkcomet, results are mostly on the default port 1604, and all matching responses are ciphertext corresponding to the V5 #KCMDDC5#-890 key.
360: Mostly on port 3333, all are 12-digit timestamps. The next most common is port 6666, where responses are mostly “000000009002”, which should be a false positive, also includes shodan results (all classified under “Application: darkcomet remote control software”, this category has no false positives). There is also an “Application: darkcomet rat” category, which includes false positives matching timestamps, presumably the matching rule is ^[0-9A-F]{12}$.
fofa: Similar to 360.
nmap: The matching rule is also ^[0-9A-F]{12}$. (Source: After the darkcomet client and master client perform a TCP handshake, the master client sends the RC4-encrypted command word ‘IDTYPE’ using a pre-set key. The default key is version-dependent. The RC4 encrypted ciphertext is 12 characters long, consisting of 0-9 and A-F.)

3 njRAT

3.1 Overview

njRAT, also known as Bladabindi, was first discovered in 2013 and developed using the .NET framework.
In October 2016, Softpedia reported that a cracked version of VMware would download and install njRAT via Pastebin, and terminating the process would crash the computer.
In January 2023, the Trojan spread in the Middle East; attackers used .cab files for “political dialogue,” which, when opened and run, would launch a .vbs script to download and install njRAT from the cloud to the local machine.

3.2 Basic Features

  1. TCP transmission, the default listening port for version 0.6.4 is 1177, different versions have different default ports.
  2. TCP flags of traffic packets involved in data transmission all include PSH.
  3. Parameters are separated by |’|’|.

3.3 Communication Characteristics

① client->server: Go online
The controlled host actively initiates a TCP connection to the master client. After the TCP three-way handshake is completed, it sends its own information to go online. Parameters are separated by “|’|’|“, and the suffix keyword is “[endof]“.

“lv”: command word;
“SGFjS2VkXzVDNjBFQUYz”: base64 encoded controlled host name, i.e., “Name” in the figure;
“WIN-GH46O995DIB”, “win7”: controlled host system information, corresponding to PC, user in the figure;
“2023-02-21”: client installation time, corresponding to Install Date in the figure;
“\x57\x69\x6e\x20\x37\x20\xbc\xd2\xcd\xa5\xc6\xd5\xcd\xa8\xb0\xe6\x20\x53\x50\x31\x20\x78\x36\x34“: controlled host operating system name, corresponding to “Operating system” in the figure;
“No”: indicates camera status, none here, corresponding to “Cam” in the figure;
“0.6.4”: njRAT version, corresponding to “Ver.” in the figure;
“5q2j5Zyo5o2V6I63IEFkYXB0ZXIgZm9yIGxvb3BiYWNrIHRyYWZmaWMgY2FwdHVyZQ==”: base64 encoded controlled host active window name, here it is “正在捕获 Adapter for loopback traffic capture” (currently capturing Adapter for loopback traffic capture), which is a Wireshark window, corresponding to “Active Window” in the figure.

Communication traffic capture:

Server console:

② server->client:
The master client responds “CAP|’|’|”, indicating capturing the victim host’s screen.

③ client->server:
The controlled host sends encoded screenshot information.

④ server->client:
The master client sends the keyword “P” to maintain the connection, and this behavior is repeated.

⑤ client->server:
The controlled host responds with the base64 encoded active window name, which is the njRAT window here, and also responds with the keyword “P” to maintain the connection, and this behavior is repeated.

4 GreameRAT

4.1 Overview

GreameRAT was released in 2012, originating from Morocco, developed using the Pascal language, compiled with the Unicode version of Delphi, and also features a modular architecture, allowing attackers to add or remove functions as needed.

4.2 Basic Features

GreameRAT uses TCP transmission. Except for version 1.9, all other default listening ports are 777, 888, 999; version 1.9 only uses port 999. In the controlled host and master client handshake communication, traffic packets containing data exchange all include PSH TCP flags, with the default key being “123456”.

4.3 Communication Characteristics

In the GreameRAT handshake, the controlled host first sends a number containing the key length n. In the test environment, 25 was found to be a fixed constant, and 25+n is the number sent, which was 31 in this handshake, with a suffix of “\x7c\0a”.

The controlled host then immediately sends key-related verification data.

Only when both data are correct will the master client respond sequentially with “\x20\x0d\x0a” to indicate reception, and data with the suffix “###@@@”, completing the handshake.

Additionally, it was found that if the controlled host sends other data in addition to the correct verification data, the master client will respond with “tentarnovamente|”, which is Portuguese for “try again”.

5 QuasarRAT

5.1 Overview

QuasarRAT was developed by GitHub user MaxXor using C#, first publicly released in July 2014, formerly known as “xRAT”. It primarily targets Windows operating systems, maintaining its persistence through scheduled tasks and registry keys.
In February 2018, researchers observed an attack campaign distributing QuasarRAT as the final payload via malicious RTF files. In May 2019, enSilo observed APT10 using PlugX and QuasarRAT to attack Southeast Asian government and private organizations. Furthermore, the notorious APT group “Transparent Tribe” (also known as Mahacala) has also consistently used this tool.

5.2 Basic Features

QuasarRAT uses TCP transmission, with a common listening port of 5678. In traffic packets related to data transmission, TCP flags all include PSH.

5.3 Communication Characteristics

The controlled host actively initiates a TCP connection. After successfully completing the TCP connection, the master client sends two responses sequentially:

“RPCMethod: preinstall” is a QuasarRAT command used to pre-install the Quasar client on the target host, and it is a communication characteristic of the QuasarRAT master client that distinguishes it from other port services.

6 XtemeRAT

6.1 Overview

XtremeRAT was released in 2010, developed using the Unicode version of Delphi, and is open source on the GitHub platform. Its main supported functions include file and process management, keylogging, service management, and registry management. It has been used by the North African Fox (APT-C-44) group in attacks targeting Arabic-speaking regions.

6.2 Basic Features

XtremeRAT uses TCP transmission, with default listening ports of 80, 81, 82, and a default password of 1234567890.

6.3 Communication Characteristics

The controlled host actively initiates a TCP handshake. After completing the TCP three-way handshake and establishing a connection, the controlled host first sends version information “myversion|3.2”.

The master client replies with the first response “X ” as a response prefix, which is an XtremeRAT communication characteristic.

Next, the master client replies with the second response. In the payload, bytes 1-8 are the key (in this example, the password is 1234), and bytes 9-16 are the zlib compressed file size (in this example, the message length is 152 bytes), both represented in little-endian. Bytes 17-18 are the zlib indicator “\x78\x01“, which is fixed in XtremeRAT communication.

6.4 Attempted to compile source code (not completed, later found the exe)

  1. Install Virtual Tree View in Delphi 2010, copy the folder to $(BDS)\Components\VST
  2. Install KOL Unicode component on Delphi 7, or install “MirrorKOLPackageD12.dpk” on Delphi 2010. Perform a normal KOL installation, then obtain Unicode version files and replace them.
    Amazingly, deleting that string of garbled characters allowed the installation.
  3. Create project:
  • Open Delphi 7 and go to: File | New | Other
  • Select the “Wizard” option. There will be a KOL palette.
  • I made some modifications, it can only be used as UNICODE, meaning, do not use “ansi” in the project.
  • Open the file “XtremeRAT.rar Components” and copy the data to the folder “$(BDS)\Components\“
  1. Install TMS components in Delphi 2010
  2. Delphi 2010 || Options | Environment Options | Delphi Options | Library - Win32 |
  1. Install AlphaControls.7.26 components. After installation, copy the files to the $(BDS)\Components\AlphaControls\D2010 folder, then
    go to Tools Options | Options | Environment Options | Delphi Options | Library - Win32 | in Delphi 2010
    $(BDS)\Components\AlphaControls\D2010

entry.title: 【学习笔记】六个RAT（远控木马）协议的通信特征
entry.content:
1 Gh0st

1.1 基本情况

Gh0stRAT最初由C.Rufus团队开发，客户端以服务的形式运行，可以随系统自启动，并通过隐藏文件和进程的方法躲避杀毒软件的检测。主要包括文件管理、系统信息收集、进程管理、屏幕截图、远程控制等模块，另外具有窃取密码、捕获网络流量和执行远程CMD命令的能力。通常通过电子邮件附件、恶意网站或软件漏洞传播。

该木马于2009年被初次发现。其3.6版本的源代码在互联网上免费开源，因此备受欢迎，很多被修改的变种都由此而来，如大灰狼。2018年监测到Iron Tiger组织在攻击活动中使用Gh0stRAT变种，2021年监测到Gh0stRAT变种通过虚假Telegram安装程序在用户计算机上植入后门。

1.2 基本特征

  1. TCP传输，默认监听端口为80，常见于8000、443 端口，默认伪装为 Microsoft Device Manager 服务
  2. 数据(payload)头用明文显示magic number。如：Gh0st、GOLDt、HEART、Level、BeiJi
  3. 固定的magic number会暴露自身版本信息
  4. 对一些协议规范外的非法请求也会发出响应，如GET / HTTP/1.0
  5. 缺少有效的包校验手段
  6. 没有合理的会话管理机制
  7. 源代码中，负责通信的发包方法是m_iocpServer()，Gh0stRAT虽然有多个变种，但此方法为各内核中核心的部分，通常不被修改，各变种有一个统一的通信特征。

1.3 通信特征

环境搭建参考：gh0st3.6 + vc6.0 + windows xp编译过程

整个通信过程中，用于传输数据的流量包的TCP标志位均包括PSH。在Gh0stRAT的握手中，被控端首先发起TCP连接请求。经过三次握手建立TCP连接后，被控端发送以“Gh0st\xae\x00\x00\x00\xe0\x00\x00\x00\x78\x9c”为前缀，包含经编码混淆后的自身系统信息的payload供上线。

主控端收到上线信息后，响应表示确认的流量包，Payload中同样以“Gh0st \xae\x00\x00\x00\xe0\x00\x00\x00\x78\x9c”为前缀。第14-15字节“\x78\x9c”为zlib的压缩头部，在Gh0st通信包中位置固定，见Gh0st大灰狼RAT家族通讯协议分析。

2 DarkcometRAT

2.1 基本情况

DarkcometRAT在2008年由Jean-Pierre Lesueur开发，又称“暗黑彗星”，除了远控木马常见的加密和混淆技术外，还带有rootkit功能，允许在系统中隐藏自身存在。2012年，木马作者停止了对于这款木马的更新，最新版本停留在5.4.1。

现在活跃的木马基本使用动态域名作为服务器域名，以“ddns.net”和“duckdns.org”为主，占比超过了50%。在腾讯反病毒实验室近年某月的威胁情报态势报告中发现，Darkcomet是该月使用最多的恶意网络协议，单日峰值达3000个。

2.2 基本特征

  1. TCP传输，默认监听端口为1604
  2. RC4算法对通信流量进行加密和解密，主控端和被控端需要使用相同的密钥接受、发送信息
  3. 使用预定义的命令字进行通信，参数之间用“|”隔开
  5. 涉及传输数据的流量包的TCP标志位均包括PSH

2.3 通信特征

完成TCP三次握手后，主控端会用预先设定的密钥，发送经RC4算法加密后的命令字“IDTYPE”。

默认密钥与版本有关，均形如“#KCMDDC𝑥#-8900123456789”， 𝑥为版本号，其中“0123456789”为用户可更改字段。

具体对应如下表所示：

随后被控端响应命令字“SERVER”密文，主控端接收后响应命令字“GetSIN”请求被控端主机信息并附带接收信息的数据库的IP地址，被控端接收后响应自身信息，完成Darkcomet握手。

2.4 平台对比

shodan：搜索darkcomet，结果多在1604默认端口，且匹配响应均为V5的#KCMDDC5#-890密钥对应密文。
360：多在3333端口，都是12位数时间戳，次多的6666端口，响应多为”000000009002”，应是误报，也包含shodan上的结果（都归在了“应用：darkcomet远控软件”里，此分类没有误报），同时还有“应用：darkcomet rat”分类，包含匹配时间戳的误报，猜测匹配规则是^[0-9A-F]{12}$。
fofa：跟360类似。
nmap：匹配规则也是^[0-9A-F]{12}$。(来源：darkcomet客户端与主控端进行tcp握手后，主控端会用预先设定的密钥，发送rc4算法加密后的命令字’IDTYPE’，默认密钥与版本有关。rc4加密后的密文长度为12位，由0-9和A-F构成。)

3 njRAT

3.1 基本情况

njRAT，别名Bladabindi，于2013年首次被发现，使用.NET框架开发。
2016年10月，Softpedia 报告称某破解版VMware下载会通过pastebin下载安装njRAT，若终止进程将会使计算机崩溃。
2023年1月，木马病毒在中东地区扩散，攻击者使用.cab文件进行“政治对话”，实际在打开运行后会启动一个.vbs脚本，从云上下载njRAT安装到本地。

3.2 基本特征

  1. TCP传输， 0.6.4版本的默认监听端口为1177，不同版本的默认端口不同
  2. 涉及传输数据的流量包的TCP标志位均包括PSH
  3. 参数之间用|’|’|隔开

3.3 通信特征

①client->server：上线
被控端主动向主控端发起TCP连接，TCP三次握手完成后，发送自身信息以完成上线。参数之间用“|’|’|”隔开，后缀的关键字为“[endof]”。

“lv”：命令字；
“SGFjS2VkXzVDNjBFQUYz”：经base64编码后的被控端名称，即图中的“Name”；
“WIN-GH46O995DIB”、“ win7”：被控端系统信息，对应图中 PC、user；
“ 2023-02-21”：客户端安装时间，对应图中 Install Date；
“\x57\x69\x6e\x20\x37\x20\xbc\xd2\xcd\xa5\xc6\xd5\xcd\xa8\xb0\xe6\x20\x53\x50\x31\x20\x78\x36\x34”：被控端操作系统名，对应图中“Operating system”；
“No”：表示摄像头状况，这里没有，对应图中“Cam”；
“0.6.4”：njRAT版本，对应图中“Ver.”；“5q2j5Zyo5o2V6I63IEFkYXB0ZXIgZm9yIGxvb3BiYWNrIHRyYWZmaWMgY2FwdHVyZQ==”： base64 编码后的被控端活动窗口名，这里是“正在捕获 Adapter for loopback traffic capture”，为 Wireshark的窗口，对应图中“Active Window”。

通信流量捕获：

server端控制台：

②server->client：
主控端响应“CAP|’|’|”， 表示捕获受害主机画面。

③client->server：
被控端发送经编码的屏幕截图信息。

④server->client：
主控端发送关键字“P”保持连接，接下来重复此行为。

⑤client->server：
被控端响应经base64 编码后的活跃窗口名，这里为 njRAT的窗口，同时响应关键字“P”保持连接，接下来重复此行为。

4 GreameRAT

4.1 基本情况

GreameRAT公布于2012年，来源于摩洛哥，使用Pascal语言开发， Delphi的unicode版本编译，同样具有模块化体系结构，允许攻击者根据自身需要添加或删除功能。

4.2 基本特征

GreameRAT 使用 TCP传输，除 1.9版本外，其余默认监听端口均为777、888、999，1.9版本的默认端口仅有999。被控端和主控端握手通信中包含数据交换的流量包，其 TCP 标志位均包括PSH，其中密钥为默认的“123456”。

4.3 通信特征

在 GreameRAT 握手中，被控端首先发送包含密钥长度n的数字，在测试环境中，发现25为固定常数，25+n即为发送的数字，在本次握手中为31，以“\x7c\0a”为后缀。

被控端紧接着发送与密钥有关的校验数据。

只有当两个数据都正确，主控端才会依次响应“\x20\x0d\x0a”表示接收，以及后缀为“###@@@”的数据，完成握手。

另外发现，若被控端除发送正确校验数据外，还发送了其他数据，主控端则会响应“tentarnovamente|”，是葡萄牙语的“tentar novamente”，意为重试。

5 QuasarRAT

5.1 基本情况

QuasarRAT 由 github 用户 MaxXor 采用 C#开发，首次公开于 2014年 7月，曾名“xRAT”，主要针对 Windows 操作系统，采用计划任务和注册表项维持自身持久存在。
2018年2月，研究人员观察到一起通过恶意RTF文件分发QuasarRAT 作为最终有效载荷的攻击活动；2019年5月，enSilo观察到APT10使用PlugX 和 QuasarRAT 对东南亚政府和私人组织发起攻击。除此之外，著名的 APT攻击组织 “摩诃草”也曾长期使用此工具。

5.2 基本特征

QuasarRAT 使用 TCP 传输，常见监听端口为 5678，与数据传输相关的流量包中，TCP标志位均包括PSH。

5.3 通信特征

被控端主动向主控端发起 TCP连接，成功完成TCP连接后，主控端依次发送两个响应：

“RPCMethod: preinstall”是 QuasarRAT 的命令，用于在目标主机上预安装Quasar客户端，是QuasarRAT主控端区别于其他端口服务的通信特征。

6 XtemeRAT

6.1 基本情况

XtemeRAT发布于2010年，使用Delphi的Unicode版本开发，在GitHub 平台上开源，主要支持功能有文件与进程管理、键盘记录、服务管理和注册表管理。曾被北非狐（APT-C-44）组织用于针对阿拉伯语地区的攻击中。

6.2 基本特征

XtremeRAT使用 TCP传输，默认监听端口为80、81、82，默认密码为1234567890。

6.3 通信特征

被控端主动发起 TCP握手，完成 TCP三次握手建立连接后，被控端首先发送版本信息“myversion|3.2”。

主控端回复第一个响应“X ”，作为响应前缀，是XtremeRAT的通信特征。

接着主控端回复第二个响应，payload 中第 1-8 字节为密钥，在此例中密码为1234，第9-16字节为zlib压缩文件大小，此例中消息长度为152字节，均为小端序表示，第17-18字节为zlib指示符“\x78\x01”，在XtremeRAT通信中固定。

6.4 尝试编译源代码（没完成，后来找到了exe）

  1. 在delphi2010安装virtual tree view，将文件夹复制到$(BDS)\Components\VST
  2. delphi7上安装KOL unicode组件，或在delphi2010上安装“MirrorKOLPackageD12.dpk”。执行正常KOL安装，然后获取Unicode版本文件并替换。
    感天动地，把那串乱码删掉就能装了
  3. 创建项目：
  • 打开delphi7并转到：文件|新建|其他
  • 选择“向导”选项。将有一个KOL调色板。
  • 我做了一些修改，只能用作UNICODE，也就是说，不要在项目中使用“ansi”。
  • 打开文件“XtremeRAT.rar Components”并将数据复制到文件夹“$（BDS）\Components\”
  1. 在delphi 2010中安装TMS组件
  2. delphi2010|| Options | Environment Options | Delphi Options | Library - Win32 |
  1. 安装AlphaControls.7.26组件，安装后将文件复制到$(BDS)\Components\AlphaControls\D2010文件夹，然后
    转到delphi 2010中的工具选项|选项|环境选项| delphi选项|库-Win32|
    $（BDS）\Components\AlphaControls\D2010

entry.title: [Study Notes] Communication Features of Six RAT (Remote Access Trojan) Protocols
entry.content: 1 Gh0st

entry.title: 【学习笔记】六个RAT（远控木马）协议的通信特征
entry.content: 1 Gh0st

entry.title: 【Paper Reading】"Detecting probe resistant proxy": Experimental Verification and Analysis
entry.content:
Introduction

When I first encountered this paper on proxy detection, I noticed a lack of Chinese discussion on it. I have compiled my notes after reading and experimenting (focusing on Tor’s obfs4), to facilitate learning and discussion for those who might need it.
(Now obfs4 is becoming obsolete, with new WebTunnels being introduced, but upgrades and iterations still take time.)

Paper Reading

Probe Selection

Our attack focuses on the observation that not responding with data is an anomaly on the internet. By sending probes consisting of popular protocols and random data, we can elicit responses from almost all endpoints (94%) in the Tap dataset of over 400k IP/port pairs.
For endpoints that do not respond, proxy-specific TCP behaviors (such as timeouts and data thresholds) differ from other servers.

Starting with 6 basic probes: HTTP, TLS, MODBUS, S7, random bytes, and an empty probe that sends no data after connection. For each probe, we record how the server responds with data (if any), when it closes the connection (if any), and how it closes the connection (TCP FIN or RST).

  • HTTP: For HTTP, we send a simple HTTP/1.1 GET request with a host header of example.com. Most servers will respond with a redirect or error page. Even non-HTTP servers may respond to this probe with error messages inherent to their protocol.
  • TLS: We send a TLS Client Hello message, typically generated by a Chromium version 71 browser. This includes popular cipher suites and extensions, and even if the server does not support TLS, it should respond with a TLS alert message.
  • Modbus: Modbus is commonly used in Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) environments for Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) and other embedded devices. We use the probe defined in ZGrab, which sends a 3-byte command to request device information descriptors from the remote host.
  • S7: S7 is a proprietary protocol used by Siemens PLC devices. Again, we use the probe defined in ZGrab, which sends a device identifier request via the COTP/S7 protocol.
  • Random Bytes: Probes with varying amounts of random bytes. Servers will fail to parse them and respond with an error message, or close the connection in a manner distinct from proxy servers.
  • Empty Probe: Does not send data after connection. Some protocols (like SSH) have the server communicate first (or simultaneously) with the client. For other protocols, implementations might have different timeouts when the client sends some initial data compared to when it sends no data.
  • DNS AXFR: Constructed a DNS AXFR (Zone Transfer) query probe based on the DNS specification.
  • STUN: Implemented a STUN probe based on the ZGrab-Golang library to probe Cisco WebEx devices.

Experimental Data

  • Proxy Endpoints: Collected over 20 obfs4 proxy endpoints from Tor’s BridgeDB.
  • Non-Proxy Endpoints: Used active network scans from ZMap and passive collection of network flow data. We used ZMap to send a SYN packet to 20,000 hosts on every TCP port (0-65535), totaling 1.3 billion probes. We found 1.5 million endpoints that responded with SYN ACK, which we labeled as the ZMap dataset. Sampled netflow data from the University of Colorado’s 10 Gbps gateway to collect endpoints. In countries without network censorship, the vast majority of traffic does not involve proxies, so the collected endpoints are primarily other services. Over a 3-day span, we collected over 550,000 server IP/port nodes from the ISP. Of these, 433,286 (79%) hosts accepted connections (responding with a TCP SYN-ACK), while most of the remainder simply timed out when connection was attempted. Subsequent scans were conducted within days of observing connections, and some servers may have moved IPs or gone offline during this time. Secondly, servers may have firewalls configured to only allow access from certain IPs, potentially blocking ZMap scans. Ultimately, over 400,000 data-sending servers were captured in this dataset.

Experimental Results

We sent 13 probes (7 from Section III-A and 6 with random data ranging from 4KB-40KB) to 1.9 million nodes and recorded whether and when the server responded with data or closed the connection. If the server closed, we recorded whether it used FIN or RST. If the server did not respond or close the connection, we timed out after 300 seconds and labeled the result as a timeout. We recorded their closing thresholds.

Since anti-probe proxies never respond with data, responsive endpoints can be discarded. In the passive Tap dataset, this eliminated 94% of hosts, leaving only 26,021 potential proxies. In the ZMap dataset, the vast majority of hosts had no data response to any probe, discarding only 1.16% of endpoints.

The reason is that among the nodes identified by ZMap, firewalls used a chaff strategy, only responding to SYN on specific subnets. 42% of nodes behaved identically, neither sending data nor closing connections to the probe. Clustering response data (response type, number of response bytes, connection close time, or timeout time). In the Tap dataset, the most popular response group contained 3% of endpoints, appearing to be TLS servers within the Cloudflare network (99.9% on endpoints with port 443). Figure 2 shows the CDFs of different response sets (ordered by popularity) for the ZMap and Tap datasets, illustrating greater diversity in the Tap dataset. The top 10 response sets accounted for over 80% of endpoints in the ZMap dataset, but only 13% of endpoints collected in Tap.

Detection Methods

  1. Buffering Thresholds: Servers will close with FIN after receiving a certain number of bytes, and may close with RST after receiving a higher number of bytes. The closing thresholds of proxy services are mostly unique. Consider a server that reads N bytes from a client and attempts to parse them as a protocol header. If parsing fails (e.g., invalid field, checksum, or MAC), the server can simply close the connection. If the client sends only N-1 bytes, the server might keep the connection open and wait for additional data before attempting to parse.

The obfs4 handshake can be up to 8192 bytes long, so the server reads this many bytes before determining the client is invalid and entering the closeAfterDelay function. This function closes the connection after a random delay (30-90 seconds), or after the server reads an additional N bytes (N randomly chosen between 0 and 8192) at server startup. However, these reads are all done using a 1448-byte buffer. This means if 8192+N bytes are sent, the obfs4 server will send a FIN, and if 8192 + N - ((8192 + N) mod 1448) + 1448 bytes are sent, it will return an RST. This is obfs4’s unique behavior.

After the paper authors communicated their research findings with torproject, obfs4proxy has fixed the closing threshold behavior in version 0.0.11.
2. Timeout Durations: Many endpoints have different timeouts based on the amount of data they receive. Hosts that do not acknowledge data are not standard TCP behavior; we retransmit, and proxies do not exhibit this behavior, so it does not affect the results.

The closing threshold was fixed, but timeout behavior can still be observed.
3. Rare Responses: Not responding to any probe is rare.

obfs4proxy Analysis

obfs4 Protocol Verification Logic

Handshake Process

sequenceDiagram    Client->>Server: SYN+ECN+CWR    Server-->>Client: SYN+ACK     Client->>Server: ACK    Client->>Server: PSH+ACK+data

Data Content

Involves algorithm: curve25519

Both client and server must have their own Keypair instances. As defined in the curve25519 package, PrivateKey is randomly generated within a certain range. According to the ECC algorithm, PublicKey can be generated from the private key by calling curve25519.ScalarBaseMult().

The Representative key is generated from the public key and can be converted back to the public key by calling extra25519.RepresentativeToPublicKey() when needed. The definition and initialization code for Keypair are located in the NewKeypair() function, defined in the ntor.go file.

Using ScalarBaseMult() from extra25519.go, generate public and representative keys from the privatekey. The representative key is obscured using Elligator 2 mapping to prevent inference of the encryption algorithm after collecting a significant number of public keys.
Algorithm detailed description

Both the client and server should keep their private keys secure and send their public keys to each other in the form of Representative keys. The obfs4 client’s connection process begins with the handshake message, so let’s examine the client’s handshake message. The overall structure of this message is as follows:

  1. Keypair.representative, with a length of 20h bytes. On the server side, it can be used to restore the client’s public key. (obfs4 bridge configuration line)
  2. Padding data using random bytes, with a size ranging from 4Dh to 1FC0h. This padding data obfuscates the size of the handshake packet, making it harder to identify.
  3. The HMAC value of the first part, Keypair.representative. Obfs4 uses SHA-256 to generate the HMAC, with a length of 20h bytes. Obfs4 only keeps the first 10h bytes as HMAC, and the remaining 10h bytes are discarded.
  4. Obfs4 uses the current system time to calculate the hour value of the UNIX Epoch time (starting from UTC 1970-01-01 Thursday 00:00:00). It calculates the HMAC value of the first three parts of the packet, plus (meaning concatenated with) the hour value in the string. Similarly, the result has a length of 20h bytes, with the first 10h bytes serving as the fourth part of the packet. (Replay protection)

obfs4proxy Source Code Structure

common/

Cryptography, socks5 and other components

internal/

Updated obfuscation algorithms

obfs4proxy/

obfs4proxy.go main function

transports/

Contains implementations of meeklite, obfs2, 3, 4, scramblesuit transport protocols

  • obfs4/
    • framing/: Data frame processing
      • handshake_ntor.go: Completes obfs4 handshake, including verification
      • obfs4.go: Handshake related functions

obfs4proxy Source Code Functions

obfs4.go

  • WrapConn(): Calls serverHandshake(). Regardless of the error returned, it calls closeAfterDelay().
  • serverHandshake(): Creates a new server handshake, setting a baseline timeout. Then, it sets a buffer for the maximum readable length (8192), reads the incoming packet, saves its content, and then parseClientHandshake() parses the content. If the parsing error is ErrMarkNotFoundYet, it continues to read packets; otherwise, the function returns an error. If parsing is successful, the buffer is cleared.

handshake_ntor.go

  • parseClientHandshake(): For too little received content / received content length not reaching the upper limit, or if MarkMAC is not found, an ErrMarkNotFoundYet error handling is prepared, allowing it to return and continue reading. All other errors close the connection. If markMAC/HMAC is not found, or packet length is non-compliant (contains data not needed for handshake), ErrInvalidHandshake is returned. For replay, ErrReplayedHandshake is returned. If calculation verification fails, ErrNtorFailed is returned.
  • closeAfterDelay(): closeAfterDelay() has been modified, fixing the vulnerability of generating FIN and RST due to thresholds. Older versions would terminate the connection after a 30s delay / receiving a certain length of data, while new versions always terminate after a 30s delay.
  • SetReadDeadline(): 30s after starting to read data, any subsequent incoming data will also return a timeout error and terminate the connection. (The paper mentions a random delay, which I couldn’t find).

obfs4proxy.go

  • serverHandler():
    Calls WrapConn(). If an error is returned, logs the handshake failure.
  • serverAcceptLoop():
    Makes the server loop to initiate handshakes with clients, calling serverHandler().

Analysis and Experiment

Paper Analysis

In the paper’s manually constructed obfs4 decision tree, it first sends 17k bytes of random data. If an RST is returned within 2s, it proceeds to the next step, sending other probes. If a FIN is returned within 30s–90s, it is identified as obfs4. The 17k data was meant to trigger the threshold issue, which has been fixed, but the delayed FIN situation still exists.

The experimental data for obfs4 bridges in the paper was too scarce, so I decided to conduct my own experimental verification. After testing several known obfs4 bridges, I coincidentally found that each bridge had a fixed timeout duration.

Conjecture: Each bridge has a fixed timeout duration. (Could the bridge change after service restart? Is the random delay time generated only once?)
I tested 6 known obfs4 bridges, sending the same data (replaying data from a successful handshake initiated by the Tor browser). This hit the “verification failed” error, leading to closeAfterDelay().
I initiated two handshakes for each bridge. For the same bridge, from two different IP clients, both handshakes returned FIN after the same duration, both falling within [30, 90]s.

Experimental Verification

  1. Test Set: After filtering the test set, I obtained over 400 active services.
  2. Judgment Criteria: TLS and HTTP probes receive no response, and 17k bytes return RST or timeout duration (returning FIN) is within [30, 90]s.
  3. Experimental Results:
    ① 80% of services were identified as obfs4 bridges. Among these, 50% returned RST, and 50% had timeout durations within the specified range.
    ② 6% of services were identified as not obfs4 bridges.
    ③ 10% of services could not be determined; sending 17k bytes immediately received a FIN, and both TLS and HTTP timed out.

Experimental Analysis

  1. 80% of bridge services met the judgment criteria, verifying the feasibility of the criteria.
  2. Among the 6% of services, sending TLS and HTTP both received responses, indicating other services could be detected. This is consistent with expectations that the provided service changed.
  3. The remaining 10% of services did not meet the judgment criteria. However, after picking a few and configuring them in the Tor browser, they could be used as bridges. After sending 20k bytes, 50% returned RST. After replaying the obfs4 handshake data, all returned FIN within [30, 90]s. It is speculated that these are bridge services that have already upgraded to newer versions. After replaying handshake data, verification fails, and they enter the delayed FIN function. However, 20k already exceeds the RST threshold observed in the paper, and later sending 30k only resulted in a few responses with RST.

Experimental Conclusions

  1. Sending sufficiently large random data immediately returns RST (fixed in obfs4proxy 0.0.11).
  2. Sending data matching the handshake length returns FIN within [30, 90]s.

Follow-up

An active collection scheme has been implemented by combining other features.

Related Resources

torproject discussion on ORPort(move to here)
In-depth paper reading
Paper discussion issue
torproject meeting notes
torproject chat logs

entry.title: 【论文阅读】《Detecting probe resistant proxy》实验验证与分析
entry.content:
引言

当时看到这篇对代理检测的论文，对它的中文讨论较少，整理了自己阅读和实验后的笔记（关注于tor的obfs4），方便有需要的同学一起学习讨论。
（现在obfs4将要过时，推出了新的WebTunnels，但升级迭代仍需时间）

论文阅读

探针选择

我们的攻击集中在这样一个观察上，即从不使用数据进行响应是互联网上的异常行为。通过发送由流行协议和随机数据组成的探针，我们可以从超过400k IP/端口对的Tap数据集中的几乎所有端点（94%）获得响应。
对于不响应的端点，代理特有的TCP行为（如超时和数据阈值）与其他服务器相比存在不同。

从6个基本探测开始：HTTP、TLS、MODBUS、S7、随机字节，以及连接后不发送数据的空探测。对于每个探测，我们都会记录服务器如何响应其响应的数据（如果有）、关闭连接的时间（如果有）以及如何关闭连接（TCP FIN或RST）。

  • HTTP：对于HTTP，我们发送一个简单的HTTP/1.1 GET请求，主机头为example.com。大部分服务器将响应重定向或错误页面进行响应。即使不是HTTP的服务器也可能使用其协议固有的错误消息对此探测作出响应。
  • TLS：我们发送一条TLS客户端Hello消息，该消息通常由Chromium版本71浏览器生成。这包括流行的密码套件和扩展，即使服务器不支持TLS，也应该响应TLS警报消息。
  • Modbus：Modbus通常用于监控和数据采集（SCADA）环境中的可编程逻辑控制器（PLC）和其他嵌入式设备。使用了ZGrab中定义的探针，它发送一个3字节的命令，从远程主机请求设备信息描述符。
  • S7：S7是西门子PLC设备使用的专有协议。再次使用ZGrab中定义的探针，该探针通过COTP/S7协议发送设备标识符请求。
  • 随机字节：不同数量随机字节的探测，服务器将解析失败，并以错误消息响应，或者以区别于代理服务器的方式关闭连接。
  • 空探针：在连接后不发送数据。一些协议（如SSH）让服务器首先（或同时）与客户端通信。对于其他协议，当客户端发送了一些初始数据时，与不发送数据时相比，实现可能具有不同的超时。
  • DNS AXFR：根据DNS规范构建了一个DNS AXFR（区域传输）查询探测。
  • STUN：实现了一个基于ZGrab-Golang库的STUN探测，用于探测Cisco WebEx设备。

实验数据

  • 代理端点：从Tor的BridgeDB收集了20多个obfs4代理端点。
  • 非代理端点：使用ZMap的主动网络扫描和网络流数据的被动收集。我们使用ZMap向每个TCP端口（0-65535）上的20000个主机发送一个SYN数据包，总共有13亿次探测。发现了150万个响应SYN ACK的端点，我们将其标记为ZMap数据集。科罗拉多大学的10 Gbps网关中采样netflow数据来收集端点。在无网络审查国家中，绝大多数流量不包含代理，所以收集到的端点主要是其他服务。在3天的时间跨度内，从ISP收集了超过550000个服务器IP/端口的节点。其中，433286（79%）个主机接受了连接（使用一个TCP SYN-ACK响应），其余的大多数在尝试连接时只是超时。后续扫描是在观察到连接后的几天内进行的，一些服务器可能在这段时间内移动了IP或离线。其次，服务器可能配置了防火墙，只允许从某些IP进行访问，可能会阻塞ZMap扫描主机。最终在这个数据集中捕获超过40万个发送数据的服务器。

实验结果

我们向190万个节点发送13个探测（第III-A节中的7个探测和6个随机数据范围为4KB-40KB的探测），并记录服务器是否和何时响应数据或关闭连接。如果服务器关闭，我们将记录它是否使用FIN或RST。如果服务器没有响应或关闭连接，我们将在300秒后超时，并将结果标记为超时。记录他们的关闭阈值。

由于抗探测代理从不响应数据，因此可以将响应的端点丢弃。在被动Tap数据集中，这排除了94%的主机，只留下26021个潜在代理。在ZMap数据集中，绝大多数主机对任何探测都没有数据响应，仅丢弃1.16%的端点。

原因是ZMap识别的节点中，防火墙使用了chaff策略，仅在特定的子网上对SYN做出响应。42%的节点行为相同，不发送数据也不关闭与探测器的连接。将响应数据聚类（响应类型、响应字节数、连接关闭时间或超时时间） 在Tap数据集中，最流行的响应组包含3%的端点，似乎是Cloudflare网络中的TLS服务器（99.9％在具有端口443的端点上）。图2显示了ZMap和Tap数据集的不同响应集（按流行程度排序）的CDF，说明Tap数据集中有更大的多样性。前10个响应集占ZMap数据集中超过80%的端点，但只有13%的端点是在Tap中收集的。

检测方法

  1. 缓冲阈值：服务器将在接收到一定数量的字节后使用FIN关闭，并可能在接收到更高数量的字节之后使用RST关闭。代理服务的关闭阈值大多数是唯一的。考虑一个从客户端读取N个字节并尝试将其解析为协议头的服务器。如果解析失败（例如无效字段、校验和或MAC），服务器可以简单地关闭连接。如果客户端仅发送N−1字节，服务器可能会保持连接打开，并在尝试解析之前等待其他数据。

obfs4握手长度可达8192字节，因此服务器在确定客户端无效并进入closeAfterDelay函数之前读取了这么多字节。此函数在随机延迟（30-90秒）后关闭连接，或在服务器启动时读取额外的N个字节（在0和8192之间随机选择的N个）后关闭。然而，这些读取都是使用1448字节的缓冲区完成的。这意味着，如果发送了8192+N字节，obfs4服务器将发送FIN，如果发送8192 + N - ((8192 + N) mod 1448) + 1448字节，则返回rst。是obfs4独有行为。

论文作者与torproject交流研究成果后，obfs4proxy已于0.0.11版本修复关闭阈值的行为。
2. 超时时间：许多端点根据其接收的数据量具有不同的超时。不确认数据的主机不是标准的TCP行为，我们会重传，代理不会有这种行为，不影响结果。

修复关闭阈值，但仍能观察到超时行为。
3. 罕见响应：不响应任何探测是罕见的。

obfs4proxy分析

obfs4协议验证逻辑

握手过程

sequenceDiagram    Client->>Server: SYN+ECN+CWR    Server-->>Client: SYN+ACK     Client->>Server: ACK    Client->>Server: PSH+ACK+data

data内容

涉及算法：curve25519

客户端及服务端都必须有自己的Keypair实例。根据curve25519包中的定义，PrivateKey在一定范围内随机生成。根据ECC算法，PublicKey可以通过调用curve25519.ScalarBaseMult()从私钥生成。

Representative秘钥根据公钥生成，在需要的时候可以调用extra25519.RepresentativeToPublicKey()函数再次转换为公钥。Keypair的定义及初始化代码位于NewKeypair()函数中，该函数在ntor.go文件中定义。

用extra25519.go里的ScalarBaseMult()，根据privatekey生成public和representative。representative通过Elligator 2映射完成混淆，避免收集到相当数量的公钥后推断出加密算法。
算法详细说明

客户端及服务端都应当保存好私钥，将公钥以Representative秘钥的形式发送给对方。Obfs4客户端的连接过程从握手报文开始，因此我们来看一下客户端的握手报文。该报文的整体结构如下所示：

  1. Keypair.representative，其长度为20h字节。在服务器端，它可以作为还原客户端的公钥。（obfs4 bridge配置行）
  2. 使用随机字节的填充数据，其数据大小范围在4Dh至1FC0h之间，该填充数据会混淆握手数据包的大小，从而使其更难识别。
  3. 第一部分Keypair.representative的HMAC值。Obfs4使用SHA-256生成HMAC，长度为20h字节。Obfs4仅将前10h字节保留为HMAC，其余10h字节将被丢弃。
  4. Obfs4使用当前系统时间来计算UNIX Epoch时间（从UTC时间1970年1月1日星期四00:00:00开始）的小时值。计算数据包中前三个部分的HMAC值，加上（指拼接起来）字符串中的小时值。同样，其结果长度为20h字节，前10h字节作为数据包的第四部分。（防重放）

obfs4proxy源代码部分结构

common/

密码学、socks5等组件

internal/

更新的混淆算法

obfs4proxy/

obfs4proxy.go 主函数

transports/

包含meeklite、obfs2、3、4、 scramblesuit传输协议的实现

  • obfs4/
    • framing/：数据帧的处理
      • handshake_ntor.go：完成obfs4握手，包括验证
      • obfs4.go：握手相关函数

obfs4proxy源代码部分函数

obfs4.go

  • WrapConn()：调用了serverHandshake()，不管返回什么错，都closeAfterDelay()。
  • serverHandshake()：新建服务端握手，设置基准超时时间。然后按能读取的最大长度（8192）设置缓冲区，读取收包，保存收包内容，接下来parseClientHandshake()解析内容。
    如果解析错误是ErrMarkNotFoundYet，继续读包，其他错误则函数返回错误。
    解析正常则清空缓冲区。

handshake_ntor.go

  • parseClientHandshake()：对于接收内容过少/接收内容长度未达上限时，或找不到MarkMAC，准备了ErrMarkNotFoundYet的错误处理，可以返回继续读。
    其他错误都关闭连接。找不到markMAC/HMAC/包长度不合规（有握手中不需要的数据），返ErrInvalidHandshake。
    重放，返ErrReplayedHandshake。
    计算验证不通过，返ErrNtorFailed。
  • closeAfterDelay()：closeAfterDelay()被修改，修复了因阈值产生FIN和RST的漏洞。旧版本会在延迟30s/接收一定长度数据后终止连接，新版本都是延迟30s终止连接。
  • SetReadDeadline()：开始读数据30s后，再有数据进入也返回超时错误，并终止连接。（论文里说有随机延迟，我没找到）

obfs4proxy.go

  • serverHandler():
    调用WrapConn()，如果返回错误，在日志中记录握手失败。
  • serverAcceptLoop():
    让服务器循环开启与客户端的握手，调用serverHandler()

分析与实验

论文简析

论文人工构造的obfs4决策树中，首先随机发送17k的数据，2s内返回RST则进入下一步，发送其他探针，30s–90s之内返回FIN，则为obfs4。17k数据是为了引发阈值问题，已被修复，但延时FIN的情况仍然存在。

论文中针对obfs4 bridge的实验数据过少，我决定自己实验验证。在测试了几个已知obfs4 bridge后，我发现巧合的，每一个bridge都有固定的超时时间。

猜想：每一个bridge都有固定的超时时间。（有可能bridge在重启服务后会改变？随机延时时间只生成一次？）
对6个已知obfs4 bridge进行测试，发送同样的data（重放tor浏览器发起的成功握手中的data），命中了验证不通过的错误，进入closeAfterDelay()。
对每个bridge发起两次握手。对于同一bridge，在两个不同ip客户端上，发起的两次握手均经过相同时间后返回FIN，时间均落在[30,90]s。

实验验证

  1. 测试集：对测试集进行验证过滤后，得到四百余存活服务。
  2. 判断条件：TLS和HTTP探针无响应，17k字节返回RST或超时时间（返回FIN）为[30,90]s。
  3. 实验结果：
    ① 80%服务判断为obfs4 bridge。其中，50%返回RST，50%超时时间在范围内。
    ② 6%服务判断不为obfs4 bridge。
    ③ 10%服务无法确定，发17k字节立刻收到FIN，TLS和HTTP均超时。

实验分析

  1. 80%bridge服务命中判断条件，验证了判断条件可行。
  2. 6%服务中，发送TLS和HTTP均有回应，可探测出其他服务，应是提供的服务发生了变化，符合预期。
  3. 剩下10%服务，未命中判断条件，但是挑了几个，在tor浏览器中设置配置行后，可以作为bridge使用。发送20k字节后，50%返回RST。重放obfs4握手data之后，全部在[30,90]s内FIN。猜测这些是已升级至新版本的bridge服务，重放握手数据后，则检验失败，进入延迟FIN函数。但是20k已经超过了论文所观察到的RST阈值，后来再发了30k，只有少数个响应RST。

实验结论

  1. 发送足够大的随机数据，马上返回RST（obfs4proxy 0.0.11版本已修复）。
  2. 发送符合握手长度数据，[30,90]s内返回FIN。

后续

结合其他特征后，已实现主动收集方案。

相关资料

torproject对于ORPort的讨论(move to here)
论文精读
论文讨论issue
torproject会议记录
torproject聊天记录

entry.title: [Paper Reading] "LZR: Identifying Unexpected Internet Services" Notes and Thoughts
entry.content:
Original Paper and Resources

Source Code
Data
Paper
PPT
Talk (YouTube)

Introduction

A Chinese-friendly reading note on the paper “LZR: Identifying Unexpected Internet Services”. It contains a large amount of machine translation and summarization of the original text, with my additions in italics.

Research Highlights

  • Investigated where services are deployed in the network and assessed the security posture of services deployed on unexpected ports.
  • Past scans assumed services were deployed on common ports, neglecting to scan unexpected service ports. Hosts responding to SYN scans did not complete the application-layer handshake, perhaps indicating hidden services or firewalls blocking further interaction.
  • By analyzing the behavior of uncompleted application-layer handshakes on unexpected service ports, firewalls and middleware were tracked to evaluate the effectiveness of anti-scanning measures.
  • Discovered that because services run on unexpected ports, L7 handshakes require pre-established secret protocol handshakes and data confirmation on each port. Services deployed on unexpected ports tend to have weaker security, such as some IoT devices.
  • Fingerprinted 88% of identifiable services with one packet, and identified 99% of identifiable unexpected services with five handshakes.
  • Sped up scanning by filtering out hosts that respond well, return SYN-ACK, but cannot complete the application-layer handshake.

Finding Real TCP Services

TCP activity does not necessarily indicate the presence of application-layer services due to security firewall tools like firewalls and middleware.

L4 and L7 Activity

Confirmed whether hosts responding to SYN-ACK packets completed the application-layer handshake for common port services. First, ZMap was used to send SYN packets, then ZGrab was used to attempt application-layer handshakes.

As a result, a significant portion of hosts responding to TCP did not complete the L7 handshake; completion was most likely on common ports and rare on uncommon ports.

The following analyzes the lack of application-layer response by examining state changes in the two scanning phases.

Connection Evasion

If firewalls indiscriminately spoof port-open packets, it would affect normal users. Therefore, firewalls need to judge whether to spoof responses according to certain rules, commonly by blacklisting IPs that send a large number of request packets.

During the TCP handshake with ZGrab, some ports did not respond to SYN-ACK, possibly due to network failure, DHCP interruption, or the scanner being blocked. Another unlinked host IP was used to scan a random ephemeral port to confirm connection evasion, revealing that 70% responded to this unlinked IP, meaning the previous IP was evaded. In the evaded IP blocks, 40% of hosts evaded /32 subnets, 10% evaded /24, and the largest was the Mexican ISP Alestra Net (ASN 11172), which evaded /20.

For IPs considered malicious, network hardware (e.g., routers) and host software (e.g., Snort) evade connections or dynamically block them. These methods prevent scanners from using a single IP to scan the network, forcing them to use multiple IPs to reach target hosts, thus increasing the difficulty for attackers. By comparing scan results from single source IPs and multiple source IPs, it was found that hosts that evaded connections did not provide legitimate services. If effective filtering can be applied during scanning, these can be safely ignored in research.

Do TCP-Responding Hosts Speak TCP?

During the second handshake with ZGrab, 96% of port services did not complete the application-layer handshake and returned SYN-ACK. Next, we explore whether hosts entered a state to exchange application-layer data or simply stopped responding after sending a SYN-ACK. The figure below illustrates how the scanner infers server states based on captured data. To enter the established state, the server only needs to send a SYN-ACK. Once the client sends an ACK, data can be transmitted normally, with the amount of data specified by the server’s window size in the SYN-ACK packet.

The server can respond with a zero-window in the SYN-ACK; when the server is ready to accept data, the client sends subsequent ACK packets to probe. ACKNOWLEDGE DATA in the figure is defined as the state where the server allows the client to send data and acknowledges the client’s data.

ACCEPT DATA captures whether the server is ready to receive data; servers reaching this state will keep the TCP connection open long enough to accept and acknowledge data. These two states are used to distinguish whether an established connection can exchange data.

A new scanner developed based on ZGrab establishes a TCP connection, sends two newlines, and infers the server’s TCP state.

After scanning random ports and IP addresses, it was found that 16% of services on common ports and 40% on rare ports could not enter the ACKNOWLEDGES DATA state.

Figure caption: (a) Percentage of ports with only SYN-ACK responses and those reaching the ACKNOWLEDGE DATA state among TCP-responding hosts; (b) Reasons why SYN-ACK returning hosts failed to enter the ACKNOWLEDGE DATA state.

Zero-Window DDoS Protection

Among services that do not accept data, 13% on common ports and 26% on rare ports return zero-window (nmap would consider a zero-window port as closed in a window scan) TCP packets and do not increase the window value to actively prevent clients from sending data. Among all scanned ports, 99.94% of hosts returning a zero-window do not increase the window value, 90% do not respond to secondary probes, and 10% reset the connection. These behaviors are network- or host-based, not service-based.

Among these, 99% of hosts that return a zero-window on one port will also return a zero-window on other ports of the same host. If a host never increases its window size in responses, then that host may never respond. Juniper’s Secure Services Gateway (SSG) proxies use this method to prevent network-based SYN cookie DDoS attacks. When a SYN-ACK packet with a zero-window is returned, after the client completes the three-way handshake with an ACK, the firewall sends a SYN packet to the server behind the firewall to establish a connection. During this time, it maintains a zero-sized TCP window with the client, and the middleware prevents the client from sending data to the server behind the firewall.

Zero-window SYN-ACK is deployed across entire subnets, with 90% of IPs exhibiting zero-window SYN-ACK within subnets larger than /24. The Florida Department of Management Services issued 16% of all zero-window SYN-ACKs across its entire network and accounted for 3% of all SYN-ACKs on random ports. Compared to subsequent RSTs, the TTL of SYN-ACKs was consistently near one hop, indicating they were issued by network devices.

Connection Interruption During Handshake

In addition to specifying a zero-window, 2% of hosts on each port never acknowledge data, despite the client returning an ACK, and cannot complete the three-way handshake. After retransmitting 7-8 SYN-ACKs, it is inferred that the server will not reach the established state. This is not just a TCP stack issue; under MCI Communication Services, IPs retransmitting SYN-ACKs on port 4567 exhibit the same behavior on other ports. The TTL of responses from real services is more than twice that of retransmitted SYN-ACKs, indicating selective packet dropping by middleware.

Interrupting connections during the handshake is a defensive action, a way for ISPs to protect users. CenturyLink (AS 209), Frontier Communications (AS 5650), and MCI Communications Services (AS 701) drop inbound traffic to 4567/TRAM post-SYN ports. Korea Telecom (AS 4766) and Axtel (AS 6503) account for 73% of connections interrupted on 7547/CWMP ports. This situation rarely occurs on common ports, with only 5% of TCP-responding hosts not accepting data and interrupting connections on port 80.

Resetting Connections

73% of common ports and 34% of rare ports that do not receive data and cannot reach the ESTABLISH state reset the connection immediately after the client completes the three-way handshake. According to RFC793, if a server does not wish to communicate with a client, it should close the TCP connection after the client acknowledges the SYN-ACK. This is how DenyHosts firewall responds to scanners. Blocking occurs more frequently on hosts running SSH than HTTP. Similar to networks sending zero-windows, this behavior is also caused by DDoS protection.

To prevent SYN flood attacks, middleware relays a SYN-ACK on behalf of the server, then establishes a connection with the server after the client completes the three-way handshake. If the server refuses the connection, the middleware terminates the client’s connection. Cisco IOS-based routers use this solution.

The likelihood of a host closing connections on common ports increases by 20%, as standard firewall policies accompanying Google load balancers accept communication on these ports by default to perform service health checks and rely on backend virtual machines to reset connections when ports are closed.

Dynamic Blocking After Handshake

Many hosts do not acknowledge any data at all and do not respond to subsequent handshakes.
To distinguish between hosts that evade the scanner after the handshake and those that simply do not acknowledge data, we simultaneously used the source IP of the initial connection and a new IP to perform an L7 handshake with hosts that initially responded but did not acknowledge data. 98% of hosts responded to the new IP, indicating they blocked the previous incoming connection. This dynamic blocking behavior only occurs after the three-way handshake, unlike the connection evasion in 2.2. The largest network with dynamic blocking after handshake is Coming ABCDE HK (AS 133201), accounting for 48% of all dynamically blocked IPs after handshake.

Other hosts that could not acknowledge data did not perform dynamic blocking because they never responded to anything after the actual handshake but always responded to all scans. Vodafone (AS 133612) and Webclassit (AS 34358) exhibited this behavior on all scanned ports, accounting for 66% of all IPs. Furthermore, TTL values did not match, indicating the presence of middleware.

Middleware Protection Capabilities

Dynamic blocking, i.e., completing the handshake without acknowledging data, is the most effective way to reduce scanning speed. This technique causes scanners to timeout by not receiving the scanner’s ACK. Zero-window SYN-ACK also causes timeouts, but zero-windows are easily filtered. Closing connections after the handshake only causes negligible slowdown.

Summary

SYN-ACK does not indicate the presence of a service. 16% of common ports and 40% of rare ports are protected by middleware and do not accept data. Below, we investigate why hosts using TCP do not always complete the L7 handshake.

Application-Layer Service Deployment

The previous section investigated L4-responding services that do not communicate with L7 services due to DoS and scanning protection. After excluding 28% of spoof services (see 3.1 for exclusion method), it was found that 27% of services on common ports and 63% of services on rare ports were not running the expected application-layer protocol.
